title: Enterprise Color Usage
description: Color guidance for professional enterprise applications

principles.title: Use Color Purposefully
principles.description: Color should communicate meaning, not decoration
rules: Status colors must be consistent across the app
Color alone shouldn't convey critical information (add icons/text)
Avoid color as the only differentiator for accessibility

principles.title: Maintain Professional Palette
principles.description: Enterprise apps should feel trustworthy and focused
rules: Limit primary color to 1-2 brand colors
Use neutral grays for text and backgrounds
Reserve saturated colors for status and CTAs only

principles.title: Ensure Contrast
principles.description: Text must be readable in all conditions
rules: Body text: minimum 4.5:1 contrast ratio (WCAG AA)

success.usage: Success messages, active status, completed tasks

warning.usage: Warning messages, pending status, near-expiry alerts

error.usage: Error messages, failed status, destructive actions

disabled.usage: Disabled buttons, inactive items, unavailable features

avoid: Pure black (#000) for text - use base-black (#1D1D1D)
Pure white (#FFF) for large backgrounds - use engineering-100
Multiple saturated colors in close proximity

darkMode.note: If supporting dark mode in enterprise app:
considerations: Test all status colors in both modes
Maintain contrast ratios in dark mode
